headline: MAKE IT OR MOVE IT
subheading: The one question that decides whether your UK medicines licence is MIA or WDA.
flux_prompt: A clean editorial split-composition pharmaceutical concept rendered in a clinical-luxury style, deep navy background divided into two quietly contrasting halves. On the left, an abstract manufacturing world: a softly glowing single glass vial taking form on a polished stainless-steel production line, fine teal vapour and a precise calibration glow suggesting a product being created and changed. On the right, a distribution world: neat geometric rows of sealed, temperature-controlled cyan shelving and a smooth conveyor path implying finished medicine simply moving unchanged through a supply chain. Between the two halves, a subtle luminous teal-to-cyan dividing seam acts as a conceptual threshold, with a faint fresh-green accent line marking the decision point and quality-control checkpoints. Shallow depth of field, soft directional lighting, frosted glass and brushed-metal textures, premium and calm, abstract not literal, 1500x900 composition, 5:3 aspect ratio, professional editorial illustration, premium quality, clean composition, modern visual storytelling, professional lighting, no text, no watermark, no logo, ultra detailed
